name: Alberta Personal Information Protection Act (PIPA)
summary: Alberta's private-sector privacy legislation, deemed substantially similar to PIPEDA by the federal government. It governs how private-sector organizations in Alberta collect, use, and disclose personal information, and applies to provincially regulated organizations operating within the province.
keyRequirements: Obtain consent before collecting, using, or disclosing personal information
Collect only the personal information reasonably necessary for the stated purpose
Provide individuals access to their personal information and the right to request corrections
Protect personal information with reasonable security safeguards
Designate a privacy officer responsible for compliance
Mandatory breach notification to the Information and Privacy Commissioner of Alberta
Conduct privacy impact assessments before implementing new systems or practices
penalties: Fines up to $100,000 CAD for individuals and $500,000 CAD for organizations per offense. Enforced by the Information and Privacy Commissioner of Alberta. No private right of action.
